raw_text:
Round 4 should be surgical and split into P0 “remove systemic nondeterminism” vs P1 “edge correctness + real bugs.” You’re exactly right that A+B+C are the leverage.

I want 5 beads this round:

3 P0 beads to collapse 17/30 failures immediately (A+B+C)

2 P0 beads to answer the only real “is the system lying?” question (D outbox) and the “are we testing the wrong concurrency guarantee?” question (E)

Leave F+G as P1 cleanup unless D exposes deeper defects.

JSON
{
  "phase_19_round4_beads": [
    {
      "id": "bd-19t",
      "title": "Serialize audit migrations in E2E harness (avoid 40P01 DDL deadlocks)",
      "description": "Prevent Postgres deadlocks (SQLSTATE 40P01) caused by concurrent audit DDL (CREATE/REPLACE FUNCTION/TRIGGER) when multiple tests run in parallel and each runs audit migrations independently. Implement a single-process migration gate for the audit DB in the e2e harness. Preferred: a DB-scoped Postgres advisory lock (pg_advisory_lock) around migration execution so even multi-process/CI parallelism is safe. Acceptable fallback: process-local Once + mutex if tests are single-process only. Ensure the lock is released even on panic/failure.",
      "how_to_think": "Think like a database concurrency engineer. Invariant: migrations run exactly once per DB per test run and never deadlock under parallel test starts. Failure mode to avoid: flakey DDL deadlocks that mask real failures and make CI non-reproducible.",
      "depends_on": [],
      "acceptance_criteria": [
        "Audit-related E2E tests no longer fail with 40P01 under parallel execution",
        "Migrations are gated by a Postgres advisory lock (or equivalent DB-scoped mechanism)",
        "Gate is robust to multiple tests starting simultaneously"
      ],
      "verification_commands": [
        "docker compose -f infra/docker/docker-compose.infrastructure.yml up -d",
        "RUST_TEST_THREADS=10 cargo test -p e2e-tests audit_* -- --nocapture",
        "No 40P01 deadlocks occur; audit suites pass"
      ],
      "estimated_complexity": 3,
      "files_involved": [
        "e2e-tests/src/db.rs",
        "e2e-tests/src/migrations.rs",
        "platform/audit/db/migrations/*",
        "e2e-tests/tests/audit_actor_propagation_e2e.rs",
        "e2e-tests/tests/audit_append_only_e2e.rs",
        "e2e-tests/tests/audit_field_diff_e2e.rs"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "bd-19u",
      "title": "Fix mutation_class inserts in tests (text literal -> enum cast)",
      "description": "Repair tests that insert text literals into the mutation_class enum column without casting, causing failures. Update inserts/fixtures to bind the correct enum type or add explicit ::mutation_class casts. Target at minimum: compliance_export_determinism and tenant_lifecycle_suspend_deprovision. Do not change production schema/types to accommodate incorrect test inserts.",
      "how_to_think": "Think like a strict SQL typing specialist. Invariant: tests insert values using correct Postgres types and never rely on implicit casts that are not guaranteed. Failure mode to avoid: loosening production types or hiding type errors that would bite in production queries/exports.",
      "depends_on": [],
      "acceptance_criteria": [
        "No failures remain due to mutation_class text->enum insert errors",
        "Fixtures consistently bind mutation_class as the enum type"
      ],
      "verification_commands": [
        "docker compose -f infra/docker/docker-compose.infrastructure.yml up -d",
        "cargo test -p e2e-tests compliance_export_determinism_e2e tenant_lifecycle_suspend_deprovision_e2e -- --nocapture",
        "All targeted tests pass; no mutation_class cast failures remain"
      ],
      "estimated_complexity": 2,
      "files_involved": [
        "e2e-tests/src/fixtures/*.rs",
        "e2e-tests/tests/compliance_export_determinism_e2e.rs",
        "e2e-tests/tests/tenant_lifecycle_suspend_deprovision_e2e.rs"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "bd-19v",
      "title": "Resolve TIMESTAMP vs TIMESTAMPTZ decode mismatch in cross-module tests",
      "description": "Fix decoding failures where tests query a TIMESTAMP (no timezone) column but decode into DateTime<Utc> (TIMESTAMPTZ). Choose the canonical approach per domain: (A) migrate the column(s) to TIMESTAMPTZ and ensure writes use UTC, or (B) update Rust decoding to use chrono::NaiveDateTime for TIMESTAMP columns. Apply consistently for the failing cross_module_payment_invoice tests and any shared fixture helpers.",
      "how_to_think": "Think like a time semantics auditor. Invariant: database timestamp types match Rust decoding types and UTC assumptions are explicit. Failure mode to avoid: silent timezone skew or brittle decoding that fails depending on DB type and driver behavior.",
      "depends_on": [],
      "acceptance_criteria": [
        "cross_module_payment_invoice failures due to TIMESTAMP/TIMESTAMPTZ mismatch are eliminated",
        "Timestamp semantics are consistent (documented UTC policy if using TIMESTAMPTZ)"
      ],
      "verification_commands": [
        "docker compose -f infra/docker/docker-compose.infrastructure.yml up -d",
        "cargo test -p e2e-tests cross_module_payment_invoice_e2e -- --nocapture",
        "Tests pass with correct timestamp decoding"
      ],
      "estimated_complexity": 3,
      "files_involved": [
        "modules/ar/db/migrations/*",
        "modules/payments/db/migrations/*",
        "modules/subscriptions/db/migrations/*",
        "modules/gl/db/migrations/*",
        "e2e-tests/tests/cross_module_payment_invoice_e2e.rs",
        "e2e-tests/src/fixtures/*.rs"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "bd-19w",
      "title": "Outbox atomicity: prove migrations applied and enforce outbox write on mutations",
      "description": "Resolve outbox atomicity violations where a mutation commits but outbox has 0 events (AR/Payments/Subscriptions). Determine whether this is (1) migrations not applied in test harness, (2) test checking wrong DB/table, or (3) real code path missing outbox enqueue inside the transaction. Fix root cause without weakening the invariant. Ensure Guard -> Mutation -> Outbox occurs in the same SQL transaction for finalize/transition operations under test.",
      "how_to_think": "Think like a transactional integrity enforcer. Invariant: every mutation that should emit an event writes exactly one outbox record in the same transaction. Failure mode to avoid: allowing state transitions without events, breaking projections, replay, audits, and cross-module consistency.",
      "depends_on": [],
      "acceptance_criteria": [
        "AR/Payments/Subscriptions outbox atomicity E2E tests pass",
        "Outbox table existence + migration application is proven in test startup logs or assertions",
        "No mutation path remains that commits without required outbox enqueue"
      ],
      "verification_commands": [
        "docker compose -f infra/docker/docker-compose.infrastructure.yml up -d",
        "cargo test -p e2e-tests ar_outbox_atomicity_e2e payments_outbox_atomicity_e2e subscriptions_outbox_atomicity_e2e -- --nocapture",
        "All outbox atomicity tests pass"
      ],
      "estimated_complexity": 5,
      "files_involved": [
        "modules/ar/src/**/*.rs",
        "modules/payments/src/**/*.rs",
        "modules/subscriptions/src/**/*.rs",
        "modules/ar/db/migrations/*",
        "modules/payments/db/migrations/*",
        "modules/subscriptions/db/migrations/*",
        "platform/event-bus/src/**/*",
        "e2e-tests/tests/*outbox*e2e.rs"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "bd-19x",
      "title": "Concurrency E2E assertions: assert guaranteed invariants, not brittle counts",
      "description": "Fix cross-module concurrency tests that assert exact success counts that are not guaranteed under timing variance (e.g., expecting exactly 1 success but observing 0 or 2). Rewrite assertions to validate the real invariants guaranteed by design: DB uniqueness holds, no illegal transitions occurred, at-most-once side effects, and final state is correct. Where exact counts are truly required, add deterministic barriers (Barrier/Notify) so the count becomes guaranteed. Keep tests strict, but only on deterministically guaranteed outcomes.",
      "how_to_think": "Think like a concurrency test designer. Invariant: tests validate correctness properties that must hold under any scheduling, and they are reproducible. Failure mode to avoid: flaky tests that fail due to scheduler timing rather than correctness regressions, or weakened tests that stop validating concurrency safety.",
      "depends_on": [],
      "acceptance_criteria": [
        "Concurrency E2E tests pass reliably across repeated runs",
        "Assertions validate uniqueness + final state + absence of illegal transitions",
        "If asserting exact counts, tests include deterministic synchronization to make counts guaranteed"
      ],
      "verification_commands": [
        "docker compose -f infra/docker/docker-compose.infrastructure.yml up -d",
        "for i in 1 2 3; do cargo test -p e2e-tests cross_module_concurrency_e2e -- --nocapture; done",
        "All runs pass; no flaky count-based failures"
      ],
      "estimated_complexity": 4,
      "files_involved": [
        "e2e-tests/tests/cross_module_concurrency_e2e.rs",
        "e2e-tests/src/concurrency.rs",
        "e2e-tests/src/fixtures/*.rs"
      ]
    }
  ],
  "priority_order": [
    "bd-19t",
    "bd-19u",
    "bd-19v",
    "bd-19w",
    "bd-19x"
  ]
}
Why this is the right Round 4

bd-19t removes the new nondeterministic deadlock class permanently.

bd-19u + bd-19v remove type/temporal mismatch failures that are pure drift.

bd-19w is the only one that might reveal a real architecture break (and must be treated as such).

bd-19x keeps concurrency tests strict but stops demanding impossible determinism.

After these 5, run the full sweep again. If anything remains, it’ll be the small “Other” bucket (NUMERIC/i64, trial_balance 400, projection pool timeout), which we can do as a final Round 5 mop-up if needed.

phase_19_round4_beads.title: Serialize audit migrations in E2E harness (avoid 40P01 DDL deadlocks)
phase_19_round4_beads.description: Prevent Postgres deadlocks (SQLSTATE 40P01) caused by concurrent audit DDL (CREATE/REPLACE FUNCTION/TRIGGER) when multiple tests run in parallel and each runs audit migrations independently. Implement a single-process migration gate for the audit DB in the e2e harness. Preferred: a DB-scoped Postgres advisory lock (pg_advisory_lock) around migration execution so even multi-process/CI parallelism is safe. Acceptable fallback: process-local Once + mutex if tests are single-process only. Ensure the lock is released even on panic/failure.
phase_19_round4_beads.how_to_think: Think like a database concurrency engineer. Invariant: migrations run exactly once per DB per test run and never deadlock under parallel test starts. Failure mode to avoid: flakey DDL deadlocks that mask real failures and make CI non-reproducible.
acceptance_criteria: Audit-related E2E tests no longer fail with 40P01 under parallel execution
Migrations are gated by a Postgres advisory lock (or equivalent DB-scoped mechanism)
Gate is robust to multiple tests starting simultaneously
verification_commands: No 40P01 deadlocks occur; audit suites pass

phase_19_round4_beads.title: Fix mutation_class inserts in tests (text literal -> enum cast)
phase_19_round4_beads.description: Repair tests that insert text literals into the mutation_class enum column without casting, causing failures. Update inserts/fixtures to bind the correct enum type or add explicit ::mutation_class casts. Target at minimum: compliance_export_determinism and tenant_lifecycle_suspend_deprovision. Do not change production schema/types to accommodate incorrect test inserts.
phase_19_round4_beads.how_to_think: Think like a strict SQL typing specialist. Invariant: tests insert values using correct Postgres types and never rely on implicit casts that are not guaranteed. Failure mode to avoid: loosening production types or hiding type errors that would bite in production queries/exports.
acceptance_criteria: No failures remain due to mutation_class text->enum insert errors
Fixtures consistently bind mutation_class as the enum type
verification_commands: All targeted tests pass; no mutation_class cast failures remain

phase_19_round4_beads.title: Resolve TIMESTAMP vs TIMESTAMPTZ decode mismatch in cross-module tests
phase_19_round4_beads.description: Fix decoding failures where tests query a TIMESTAMP (no timezone) column but decode into DateTime<Utc> (TIMESTAMPTZ). Choose the canonical approach per domain: (A) migrate the column(s) to TIMESTAMPTZ and ensure writes use UTC, or (B) update Rust decoding to use chrono::NaiveDateTime for TIMESTAMP columns. Apply consistently for the failing cross_module_payment_invoice tests and any shared fixture helpers.
phase_19_round4_beads.how_to_think: Think like a time semantics auditor. Invariant: database timestamp types match Rust decoding types and UTC assumptions are explicit. Failure mode to avoid: silent timezone skew or brittle decoding that fails depending on DB type and driver behavior.
acceptance_criteria: cross_module_payment_invoice failures due to TIMESTAMP/TIMESTAMPTZ mismatch are eliminated
Timestamp semantics are consistent (documented UTC policy if using TIMESTAMPTZ)
verification_commands: Tests pass with correct timestamp decoding

phase_19_round4_beads.title: Outbox atomicity: prove migrations applied and enforce outbox write on mutations
phase_19_round4_beads.description: Resolve outbox atomicity violations where a mutation commits but outbox has 0 events (AR/Payments/Subscriptions). Determine whether this is (1) migrations not applied in test harness, (2) test checking wrong DB/table, or (3) real code path missing outbox enqueue inside the transaction. Fix root cause without weakening the invariant. Ensure Guard -> Mutation -> Outbox occurs in the same SQL transaction for finalize/transition operations under test.
phase_19_round4_beads.how_to_think: Think like a transactional integrity enforcer. Invariant: every mutation that should emit an event writes exactly one outbox record in the same transaction. Failure mode to avoid: allowing state transitions without events, breaking projections, replay, audits, and cross-module consistency.
acceptance_criteria: AR/Payments/Subscriptions outbox atomicity E2E tests pass
Outbox table existence + migration application is proven in test startup logs or assertions
No mutation path remains that commits without required outbox enqueue
verification_commands: cargo test -p e2e-tests ar_outbox_atomicity_e2e payments_outbox_atomicity_e2e subscriptions_outbox_atomicity_e2e -- --nocapture

phase_19_round4_beads.title: Concurrency E2E assertions: assert guaranteed invariants, not brittle counts
phase_19_round4_beads.description: Fix cross-module concurrency tests that assert exact success counts that are not guaranteed under timing variance (e.g., expecting exactly 1 success but observing 0 or 2). Rewrite assertions to validate the real invariants guaranteed by design: DB uniqueness holds, no illegal transitions occurred, at-most-once side effects, and final state is correct. Where exact counts are truly required, add deterministic barriers (Barrier/Notify) so the count becomes guaranteed. Keep tests strict, but only on deterministically guaranteed outcomes.
phase_19_round4_beads.how_to_think: Think like a concurrency test designer. Invariant: tests validate correctness properties that must hold under any scheduling, and they are reproducible. Failure mode to avoid: flaky tests that fail due to scheduler timing rather than correctness regressions, or weakened tests that stop validating concurrency safety.
acceptance_criteria: Concurrency E2E tests pass reliably across repeated runs
Assertions validate uniqueness + final state + absence of illegal transitions
If asserting exact counts, tests include deterministic synchronization to make counts guaranteed
verification_commands: for i in 1 2 3; do cargo test -p e2e-tests cross_module_concurrency_e2e -- --nocapture; done
All runs pass; no flaky count-based failures
